laws.title: Offence Relating to Public Servant being Bribed
laws.meaning: A public servant who obtains or accepts gratification other than legal remuneration as motive or reward commits an offence.

laws.title: Appeal
laws.meaning: If information is not provided within 30 days or is refused, applicant can file first appeal and then second appeal to Central / State Information Commissioner.
laws.punishment: Rs. 250 per day penalty on PIO up to Rs. 25,000

laws.title: Writ Jurisdiction of High Court
laws.meaning: High Courts can issue writs including Mandamus (to compel public authority), Certiorari (to quash illegal orders), Prohibition, Habeas Corpus, and Quo Warranto.

laws.title: Prosecution of Judges and Public Servants
laws.meaning: Prior government sanction is required before prosecuting a judge or public servant for acts done in discharge of official duty.

laws.title: Functions of the Commission
laws.meaning: NHRC can inquire into complaints of violations of human rights committed by state or its agents, including police.

evidence_list.name: Written Complaint / Application Made to Authority
Response or Rejection Letter from Authority
Audio / Video Recording of Bribe Demand

questions.text: What type of administrative complaint is this?
Which government department / authority is involved?
Have you made a formal written complaint to the authority?
What was the response from the authority?
Have you filed an RTI to get relevant information?

recommended_actions: File formal complaint with designated grievance officer of the department
Lodge complaint on CPGRAMS (Centralized Public Grievance Redress) portal
File RTI application to obtain relevant government documents
Approach Lokayukta / Anti-Corruption Bureau with evidence of bribery
File complaint with Vigilance Department if public servant involved
Approach High Court with Writ of Mandamus if authority is not acting
File complaint with National Human Rights Commission for rights violations
Approach Election Commission of India for election-related complaints
